name: Don't Buy This Jacket
context: Patagonia placed a full-page ad in the NYT on Black Friday telling consumers NOT to buy their jacket.
insight: The most sustainable product is the one you don't buy.
solution: Full-page NYT ad with 'Don't Buy This Jacket' headline. Launched Common Threads Initiative for repair, reuse, recycle.
results: Sales increased 30%. Brand trust hit record highs. Pioneered anti-consumerism marketing.

name: Real Beauty Campaign
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, Effie Grand Prix
context: Only 2% of women worldwide considered themselves beautiful.
insight: Beauty advertising was making women feel worse. A brand celebrating real beauty could transform the category.
solution: Multi-year campaign featuring real women. 'Real Beauty Sketches' became most-watched ad of all time.
results: Sales grew from $2.5B to $4B. Changed beauty industry advertising standards.

name: Dream Crazy ft. Kaepernick
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, Emmy Award
context: Colin Kaepernick was blacklisted from the NFL for protesting racial injustice.
insight: Believe in something, even if it means sacrificing everything.
solution: Two-minute brand film narrated by Kaepernick. Outdoor campaign with just his face and the Nike swoosh.
results: $6B brand value increase. 31% online sales spike. Cultural watershed moment.

name: Fearless Girl
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix x4, D&AD Black Pencil
context: Only 5% of Fortune 500 CEOs were women.
insight: One small statue could challenge the most powerful symbol of capitalism.
solution: Bronze statue of a girl facing Wall Street's Charging Bull, placed on International Women's Day.
results: 1,000+ companies changed board composition. $7.4B in earned media.

name: The E.V.A. Initiative
context: Crash test dummies modeled on male bodies. Women 47% more likely to be seriously injured.
insight: Car safety had a gender bias problem nobody was talking about.
solution: Volvo opened 40 years of safety research to all manufacturers. E.V.A. (Equal Vehicles for All).
results: Changed industry safety standards. Massive global media coverage.

name: Buy Back Friday
context: Black Friday drives excessive consumption.
insight: Instead of discounting new products, reward customers for bringing back old ones.
solution: Customers sold back used IKEA furniture for store credit. Resold in second-hand section.
results: 100,000+ pieces of furniture given second life. Significant media coverage.

name: Parley for the Oceans
context: 8 million tons of plastic enter oceans annually.
insight: Threat = raw material. Ocean plastic is a resource for making better products.
solution: Ultraboost shoes made from recycled ocean plastic. Scaled to millions of pairs.
results: 30M+ Parley shoes sold. Thousands of tons of plastic diverted from oceans.

name: Trash Isles
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix (PR), D&AD Gold
context: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is the size of France.
insight: If pollution is a landmass, it deserves the rights of a country.
solution: Applied to UN to recognize garbage patch as country 'Trash Isles'. Al Gore became first citizen.
results: UN attention. 200+ countries saw petition. Changed conversation about ocean plastic.

name: #LikeAGirl
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, D&AD Black Pencil
context: 'Like a girl' was universally used as an insult.
insight: When did 'like a girl' become an insult instead of a statement of strength?
solution: Documentary-style film showing how adults vs. young girls interpret 'like a girl'.
results: 85M+ YouTube views. 76% of women no longer saw phrase as negative. Sales grew double digits.

name: True Name
context: Transgender people forced to use deadname on credit cards.
insight: Every transaction is a potential moment of misgendering and humiliation.
solution: Cardholders display chosen True Name without legal name change required.
results: Adopted by major banks. 68% increase in brand favorability among LGBTQ+.

name: The Moldy Whopper
context: Fast food associated with artificial preservatives.
insight: Showing a rotting burger proves it's made with real ingredients.
solution: Time-lapse of Whopper decomposing over 34 days. 'The beauty of no artificial preservatives.'
results: 8.5B impressions. Biggest brand sentiment swing for BK.

name: Xbox Adaptive Controller
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, D&AD Impact
context: 400M gamers worldwide have disabilities. Standard controllers unusable for many.
insight: When everybody plays, we all win.
solution: Fully customizable controller for gamers with limited mobility. Super Bowl ad with real gamers.
results: Sold out immediately. Changed accessibility standards across gaming.

name: Good Vibes
context: Deafblind people are among the most isolated in society.
insight: A smartphone can translate between the seeing-hearing world and deafblind world.
solution: App converting text to Morse-like vibrations. Deafblind users communicate via haptic feedback.
results: Changed lives of deafblind community. Open-sourced for other platforms.

name: Shutter Ads
context: COVID forced bars to close. Small owners faced financial ruin.
insight: Every closed shutter is empty advertising space.
solution: Heineken rented shuttered storefronts as ads, paying bar owners their monthly rent.
results: Thousands of bars supported. Brand love scores increased.

name: Post-Gangsta Oat Milk
context: Plant-based milk seen as niche and boring.
insight: Make sustainability the punk rock choice.
solution: Provocative branding, packaging as manifesto, CEO singing badly in Super Bowl ad.
results: Revenue grew from $200M to $700M. IPO at $10B. Changed food brand communication.

name: Project Sunroof
context: Homeowners didn't know if solar panels would work on their roof.
insight: The biggest barrier to solar isn't cost — it's uncertainty.
solution: Google Maps data to calculate solar potential of every rooftop. Free tool with savings estimates.
results: Millions of rooftops analyzed. Significant increase in solar installations.

name: Move to Zero
context: Sportswear has massive carbon footprint.
insight: Zero is the ultimate finish line. Making sustainability a race reframes the conversation.
solution: Platform covering zero carbon/waste. Space Hippie shoes from factory scraps. Refurbished program.
results: 78% renewable energy. 100M+ pounds waste diverted. Space Hippie became cultural moment.

name: Green Hydrogen
context: Heavy industry accounts for 30% of global CO2.
insight: Energy transition isn't just about electricity — it's about replacing fossil fuels where batteries can't go.
solution: Industrial-scale electrolyzers producing green hydrogen. Partnered with steel and chemical industries.
results: Gigawatt-scale projects. Steel decarbonization partnerships. New industrial value chains.

name: Greener Stores
context: 35,000+ stores meant enormous environmental footprint.
insight: A framework that transforms 35,000 stores changes the industry.
solution: 25-standard Greener Store framework. Partnered with WWF for verification.
results: 10,000+ stores certified. 30% energy reduction. Framework shared as open-source.

name: The Ocean Cleanup
context: Ocean plastic cleanup considered impossible at scale.
insight: Let the ocean do the work. Passive systems using currents are exponentially more efficient.
solution: Floating barriers using ocean currents. Interceptor systems in rivers.
results: 500,000+ kg plastic removed. Revenue from recycled products. $100M+ raised.
